violations.description: Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitize solution for mechanical warewashing machine was not maintained at the proper concentration. Dish machine chlorine sanitizer was found at 0ppm during inspection. Chlorine sanitizing solutions should be at least 50ppm. -Corrected during inspection.

General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. Observed food employee leave the facility to deliver a to-go meal outside. Upon returning, she did not wash her hands before handling foods for additional to-go orders. Hands should be washed upon returning to the kitchen, before beginning food preparation.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. Wiping cloths found stored in a chlorine solution at 50ppm. Chlorine solutions for storing wiping cloths should be at least 100ppm. - Observed new buckets being filled and tested at 100ppm. Discussed making sure packet of solution is fully dissolved before adding towels so the chlorine is not absorbed into the towels instead.

Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitize solution for mechanical warewashing machine was not maintained at the proper concentration. Dish machine did not register chlorine sanitizer during inspection. Chlorine sanitizers should be maintained at 50ppm for sanitizing equipment.

Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not stored in a clean, dry manner to protect from contamination. Clean pans on the shelf next to the freezer with chicken nuggets and burger patties were found with crumbs from the chicken nuggets on them. Clean equipment should be stored in a clean location where they will not be contaminated.
